title: Birdsong
premise: A place life has quietly taken back.

stages.narration: Green has come up through a split in the tarmac, and there are birds in it — actual birds, bickering over something, wholly indifferent to you. For a little while it is only a morning, and nothing else.

choices.label: Sit a while and let it be ordinary
Mark it — a good place to breathe, later
You don't have the hours to spare — press on

notes: living-thing, dawn/morning. Sitting is real recovery for real time; marking banks the spot for less relief; pressing on saves the time but the tension stays wound. FR-ENC-01 phase-fitted ambient.
